headline: Property Overhang Reaches 37,000 Unsold Units Worth RM23B
context: NAPIC data showed Malaysia's residential property overhang hit 37,142 unsold completed units valued at RM23.4 billion by Q3 2025, concentrated in Johor, Selangor, and Penang.

cards.big: 37,000 homes sit empty while 1.2 million families can't afford their first property
cards.sub: 72% of unsold units priced above RM500,000 — median household income supports only RM350,000 mortgage

cards.big: Developers launched 28,000 units above RM500,000 in 2025 but only 6,200 units below RM300,000
cards.sub: Supply-demand mismatch means overhang grows by RM3 billion annually despite cooling measures

cards.big: Average Malaysian needs 8.1 years of gross income to buy a median-priced home — up from 4.4 in 2010
cards.sub: Homeownership rate among under-35s dropped to 38% from 52% a decade ago

cards.big: Housing Ministry approved 12 developer bailout extensions in 2025 despite overhang being developer-driven
cards.sub: Bumiputera quota release mechanism took average 14 months — unsold units sit idle while buyers wait

cards.big: The overhang isn't a market failure — it's developers building for profit margins, not for people who need homes
Should developers face a 5% annual tax on unsold completed units after 24 months to force price correction?
